title: Doomsday
weakest_section: 19:00-21:30 range with squash scene physical comedy

jokes.text: Hey, everybody! It's closing time! You all gotta go home but you can stay here.
jokes.setup: Manager announces closing time
jokes.punchline: Contradicts himself immediately - you can stay after being told to go home
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy-style managerial confusion. Good character integration but fairly predictable setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Otherwise, you go home and the night just feels like more day, it's weird.
jokes.setup: Explaining why offices need end-of-day traditions
jokes.punchline: Absurd logic about night feeling like day without ritual
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing pseudo-philosophical observation that fits Andy's character.

jokes.text: Oh, my boss is singing closing time. Maybe that's what you're hearing.
jokes.setup: Talking head segment addressing the audience
jokes.punchline: Pretends the obvious singing we all hear might be confusing to viewers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim meta-humor, acknowledging the documentary format with dry understatement.

jokes.text: Let's see. Andy has been manager for 105 days. Which means I've heard closing time 105 times. Still don't know the words.
jokes.setup: Calculating how long Andy's been manager
jokes.punchline: Despite hearing it daily, still doesn't know the lyrics
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and relatable workplace humor about repetitive boss behavior.

jokes.text: ♪ Ah wah dah wah home and home and home ♪
jokes.setup: Claims he doesn't know the words to Closing Time
jokes.punchline: Proves it by singing complete nonsense lyrics
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual/audio payoff to the setup. The nonsense lyrics are genuinely funny.

jokes.text: You know what? Fine! I try to start fun traditions for you guys, but if you don't wanna sing, no traditions!
jokes.setup: Andy's daily singing ritual
jokes.punchline: Petulant reaction when others don't participate enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy tantrum behavior, but fairly predictable workplace boss moment.

jokes.text: ♪ Closing time ♪ ♪ every new beginning ♪ I never heard that song before. And once I heard it, I did not care for it. But that songs means it's time to go home. Now it's my favorite song.
jokes.setup: Stanley explaining his relationship with Closing Time
jokes.punchline: Complete 180 from disliking it to loving it purely for practical reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley logic - purely practical relationship with workplace annoyances. Great character integration.

jokes.text: - Who's your favorite iron chef? - This report is atrocious.
jokes.setup: Robert asks casual question about Iron Chef
jokes.punchline: Dwight completely ignores it to focus on business report
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Robert's casual weirdness and Dwight's intensity.

jokes.text: Did you need something from us? Yes. Your attention. Uh, because... No. That is all.
jokes.setup: Andy asks what Robert needs from them
jokes.punchline: Robert's response trails off awkwardly with no real purpose
jokes.explanation: Captures Robert's bizarre management style but fairly weak structurally.

jokes.text: Last night, I dreamed that the number two was the most valued number in the world. The vice president had all the power, athletes fought for silver medals. Women were considered the best gender. And stadiums of fans shouted 'we're number 2!'
jokes.setup: Dwight describing a dream about being number two
jokes.punchline: Absurd world where second place is most valued, including weird gender hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Dwight logic with great escalation. The 'women were considered the best gender' line is particularly funny.

jokes.text: As with all my dreams, I'm guessing it was about my fear of immigrants.
jokes.setup: Complex dream about hierarchy and status
jokes.punchline: Reduces it to completely unrelated xenophobia
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight non-sequitur. Takes elaborate dream interpretation and reduces it to casual bigotry with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Chalk that one up to tweedle Dee and tweedle dum out there. Who are they? They're both Kevin.
jokes.setup: Dwight mentions tweedle dee and tweedle dum
jokes.punchline: They're both Kevin - impossible but perfectly describes Kevin's competence level
jokes.explanation: Brilliant impossible logic that perfectly captures Kevin's incompetence. Economy is perfect - maximum funny per word.

jokes.text: Oscar is the sex and the city gang.
jokes.setup: Dwight describing office workers using metaphors
jokes.punchline: Oscar somehow represents the entire Sex and the City cast
jokes.explanation: Random but fits Dwight's weird metaphorical thinking. Less impactful than the Kevin line.

jokes.text: Sometimes I feel like you don't know me at all. I would agree with that.
jokes.setup: Dwight says Robert doesn't know him
jokes.punchline: Robert immediately agrees completely
jokes.explanation: Good Robert California deadpan delivery. Perfect character moment showing his disconnect from employees.

jokes.text: When I come back next week and this report show me no mistakes, we can talk about names all day. Our favorite names, silly made-up names, normal names said in a silly voice. Wouldn't that be nice?
jokes.setup: Robert demanding no mistakes in next report
jokes.punchline: Bizarre reward of discussing names in various silly ways
jokes.explanation: Peak Robert California weirdness - treating silly name games as a meaningful workplace reward.

jokes.text: And you can't have a favorite iron chef. It depends entirely on the secret ingredient.
jokes.setup: Earlier question about favorite Iron Chef
jokes.punchline: Robert's overly analytical take on cooking show preferences
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Robert's need to intellectualize everything, even cooking shows.

jokes.text: Sometimes I feel you don't know food at all.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Iron Chef preferences
jokes.punchline: Condescending judgment about someone's food knowledge over TV show
jokes.explanation: Typical Robert condescension delivered with perfect seriousness about trivial topic.

jokes.text: - Maybe justine. - Bah! Nope. Not justine. Never justine. - Is that off again? - Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Jim suggests someone named Justine for activity
jokes.punchline: Pam's strong reaction reveals relationship drama
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship humor but Pam's emphatic reaction sells it.

jokes.text: Are you really this lazy? I'll be there.
jokes.setup: Val calls out laziness about safety meeting
jokes.punchline: Gabe immediately caves to impress her
jokes.explanation: Shows Gabe's desperation to impress but fairly predictable setup.

jokes.text: Mondays suck. Yep.
jokes.setup: Gabe trying to make conversation
jokes.punchline: Painfully basic attempt at connection with minimal response
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Gabe's awkward flirting attempts but very basic execution.

jokes.text: Ah, I should've used a shorter string. Never mind. I know it by heart.
jokes.setup: Dwight has prepared presentation materials
jokes.punchline: String is comically too long, but he's so prepared he doesn't need notes anyway
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup showing Dwight's over-preparation and confidence.

jokes.text: Do not go there! You're the deuce I never wanna drop.
jokes.setup: Andy calling Dwight his number two
jokes.punchline: Andy's crude pun about not wanting to drop a deuce
jokes.explanation: Lowbrow pun that fits Andy's inappropriate humor style but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I'm not technically seeing her, but I've seen her-- with the eyes-- and there was attraction in at least one direction. So...
jokes.setup: Gabe explaining his relationship with Val
jokes.punchline: Admits it's completely one-sided and he's just looked at her
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Gabe's delusion about his romantic prospects.

jokes.text: It's gonna be hot and heavy, and I don't want a bunch of bureaucratic red tape wrapped around my jock, you know?
jokes.setup: Gabe explaining why he needs paperwork ready
jokes.punchline: Gross overshare about his non-existent sex life using sports metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Gabe inappropriate oversharing that makes everyone uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Yeah, we had a whole conversation about mondays.
jokes.setup: Toby asks if Gabe has talked to Val
jokes.punchline: Gabe thinks their earlier two-word exchange was a 'whole conversation'
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Gabe's delusion about their connection. Great economy.

jokes.text: Toby, I'm gonna tell you her last name tomorrow because she's gonna be screaming it tonight. She's gonna be screaming her own last name?
jokes.setup: Gabe brags about knowing her last name after sex
jokes.punchline: Toby points out the logical flaw in his sex boast
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline with Toby's logical observation deflating Gabe's bravado. Great character dynamic.

jokes.text: Five strikes in a day equals a homerun. One home run and you're out.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his accountability system
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards baseball logic where home runs are bad
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Dwight logic mixing baseball metaphors incorrectly but with complete confidence.

jokes.text: something bad happens, like we block mindsweeper.
jokes.setup: Explaining consequences of mistakes
jokes.punchline: Blocking Minesweeper presented as equivalently bad to being fired
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor showing Andy's perspective on workplace consequences.

jokes.text: "Robert's favorite songs, Creep by TLC, Creep by Radiohead." Remember that one, Jim?
jokes.setup: Reading embarrassing emails about Robert
jokes.punchline: Specific observation about Robert liking both songs called 'Creep'
jokes.explanation: Funny specific detail that feels like something the characters would actually notice and mock.

jokes.text: "there's no way he hasn't strangled at least one stripper." Oscar.
jokes.setup: Reading mean emails about Robert
jokes.punchline: Oscar's surprisingly dark speculation about murder
jokes.explanation: Shocking line from typically mild-mannered Oscar. Great contrast with his character.

jokes.text: "He eats his yogurt like he is punishing it for disappoint him." Kelly.
jokes.setup: More embarrassing emails being read
jokes.punchline: Specific observation about Robert's yogurt-eating style
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and weird observation that perfectly captures both Kelly's voice and Robert's intensity.

jokes.text: "P.S. We should kill him."
jokes.setup: Reading increasingly harsh emails
jokes.punchline: Casual postscript suggesting murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and economy. The casual 'P.S.' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Wait, so you installed a doomsday device? No, it's an accountability booster.
jokes.setup: Jim calling it a doomsday device
jokes.punchline: Dwight's insistence on euphemistic corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Good running gag throughout episode. Dwight's refusal to acknowledge reality is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: They're making me out to be a Bond villain. I like to think of myself as a brilliant scientist who will stop nothing to remake the world... Like... Not Dr. Moreau. Someone good. Dr. Frankenstein. Dr. Jekyll. Not them. Doctor...
jokes.setup: Dwight defending himself against villain accusations
jokes.punchline: Lists only evil doctors while trying to think of good ones
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - completely lacks self-awareness while proving everyone's point about him being a villain.

jokes.text: Oh! There's one mistake. Now we only have four strikes left until a homerun.
jokes.setup: Oscar deliberately makes a mistake to test the system
jokes.punchline: Uses Dwight's backwards baseball terminology earnestly
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier absurd terminology, showing Oscar playing along with the system.

jokes.text: It's not a race. Thirrr-- Third.
jokes.setup: People calling out positions in safety meeting
jokes.punchline: Darryl starts to say 'third' dramatically but catches himself
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy moment showing workplace competitiveness.

jokes.text: It's for protecting my ass when you suckers lift more than you can handle.
jokes.setup: Explaining what the support belt is for
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of coworkers' lifting abilities
jokes.explanation: Standard workplace humor but delivered with good attitude.

jokes.text: Look at this. This is enormous. Oh.
jokes.setup: Gabe commenting on the size of a support belt
jokes.punchline: Awkward pause as he realizes he's made an inappropriate size comment
jokes.explanation: Typical Gabe inappropriate comment with awkward recovery.

jokes.text: I got the sense that Val enjoys a good put-down. Considering that's the only thing I know about her, I will be milking that hard.
jokes.setup: Gabe explaining his flirting strategy
jokes.punchline: Admits he knows nothing about her but will overuse his one insight
jokes.explanation: Good insight into Gabe's desperate flirting strategy and social awkwardness.

jokes.text: The Michelin man called. He wants his cummerbund back.
jokes.setup: Gabe attempting to insult someone about the belt
jokes.punchline: Awkward mashup of Michelin man reference with formal wear terminology
jokes.explanation: Typical Gabe awkward attempt at humor - tries too hard and uses pretentious vocabulary.

jokes.text: When the stake are this high, there's only one computer that I trust, and it's powered by thai food and spanish reds.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining why he's not using a calculator
jokes.punchline: Claims his brain powered by specific foods is more reliable than a computer
jokes.explanation: Great Kevin line showing his delusional confidence in his abilities while revealing his diet.

jokes.text: Try something like, um, z64$8. Not that exactly, Jim. Something like that.
jokes.setup: Pam suggesting password ideas
jokes.punchline: Gives specific example then immediately says not to use it exactly
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about password security habits, but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: How about 'Scranton strangler 666'?
jokes.setup: Jim guessing Dwight's password
jokes.punchline: Combines local serial killer with devil number for Dwight
jokes.explanation: Good callback and perfectly captures what Jim thinks Dwight's password would be.

jokes.text: What's Dwight's mother's name? Um, Hedda. No.
jokes.setup: Trying to guess password based on personal info
jokes.punchline: Jim confidently gives wrong name for Dwight's mother
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim doesn't know Dwight as well as he thinks, but fairly minor moment.

jokes.text: A $100 bill. Should cover it, I think.
jokes.setup: Gabe offering to pay for coffee
jokes.punchline: Massively overpays to show off wealth
jokes.explanation: Classic Gabe showing off behavior, but fairly predictable execution.

jokes.text: Don't bring me any of that caramel soy latte crap, okay? I want a decaf frappuccino. Val? Actually, Val, why don't you come with?
jokes.setup: Gabe being demanding about coffee order
jokes.punchline: Immediately changes to asking Val to come along
jokes.explanation: Shows Gabe's transparent attempt to spend time with Val but execution is predictable.

jokes.text: Well, I was saving this for my retirement, which I guess is today.
jokes.setup: Stanley commenting on the fifth mistake being made
jokes.punchline: Dry acceptance that he's about to be fired
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley response - dry, resigned, and perfectly in character. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Can you tell me now where paper comes from? Uh...The man tree puts his penis--
jokes.setup: Dwight asking Kevin about his fake project
jokes.punchline: Kevin starts explaining paper production in sexual tree terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Kevin logic combining biology lesson with completely wrong tree sexuality. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: I'll make sure to write you a glowing reference. Glowingly negative.
jokes.setup: Dwight offering to write references
jokes.punchline: Clarifies that 'glowing' means negatively glowing
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that shows Dwight's vindictive nature and love of being technically correct.

jokes.text: Pam, you should come with me. Dwight really likes you and...Your breasts are enormous. That can help us.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining why Pam should come negotiate with Dwight
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate comment about using her body to persuade Dwight
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin inappropriate comment, but relies on basic 'Kevin says inappropriate thing' formula.

jokes.text: Guys, come on. I'm right here.
jokes.setup: Kevin making inappropriate comments about her body
jokes.punchline: Pam's understated reaction to being objectified
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Pam's discomfort with appropriate understatement.

jokes.text: What does it look like I'm doing? Digging a grave for a horse.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what Dwight is doing
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact response about digging horse grave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight absurdism delivered with complete seriousness. Great visual comedy setup.

jokes.text: If you hit another horse, you dug too far.
jokes.setup: Offering help with digging horse grave
jokes.punchline: Practical advice implying multiple buried horses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation suggesting a horse cemetery on the property. Perfect economy and timing. Peak Dwight absurdism.

jokes.text: Oh, wow. I forgot how pretty your house is. This is the newest addition built by Erasmus Schrute in 1808. It doubled as a tuberculosis recovery room until 2009.
jokes.setup: Pam compliments Dwight's house
jokes.punchline: Dwight reveals the 'newest' addition is 200+ years old and recently housed TB patients
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with 'newest' being 200 years old, plus disturbing tuberculosis detail. Perfect Schrute family history.

jokes.text: That's too much doodle and not enough lab. Yeah, when are they gonna do a labradoodle that's just lab?
jokes.setup: Discussion about labradoodles
jokes.punchline: Kevin wants a labradoodle with no poodle, just lab
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay humor about dog breeds but nothing exceptional.

jokes.text: So tonight I was thinking I'm gonna go to the cemetery, I'm gonna drink a little wine, and I thought maybe you'd like to come with me.
jokes.setup: Gabe asking Val on a date
jokes.punchline: Proposes cemetery wine drinking as romantic activity
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Gabe date idea. Cemetery wine is such a specific creepy choice that fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: I could quit. Problem solved.
jokes.setup: Val says she doesn't date coworkers
jokes.punchline: Gabe immediately offers to quit his job
jokes.explanation: Good Gabe desperation moment but fairly predictable response to rejection.

jokes.text: I know how to save the company, everyone. Just write a petition. Get everyone's signature, including our clients. March down to Florida... And shove it up your butt.
jokes.setup: Stanley proposing serious solution to company problems
jokes.punchline: Classic 'shove it up your butt' misdirection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley misdirection callback. Sets up serious business solution then delivers his signature line.

jokes.text: Oh, Pam, you got something on your shirt. Oh! Oh, well. Pobody's nerfect, right?
jokes.setup: Pam spills something on her shirt
jokes.punchline: Says 'pobody's nerfect' instead of 'nobody's perfect'
jokes.explanation: Cute spoonerism that becomes a minor running gag in the scene.

jokes.text: Did you just have a stroke, Pam? It's nobody's perfect. Nice stroke, Pam.
jokes.setup: Pam says 'pobody's nerfect'
jokes.punchline: Dwight assumes she had a medical emergency and mocks her for it
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight insensitivity humor - immediately assumes medical emergency and is unsympathetic.

jokes.text: Sive drafely. Isn't it supposed to be drive safely?
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to say 'drive safely'
jokes.punchline: Makes same spoonerism mistake as Pam's 'pobody's nerfect'
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the spoonerism is contagious, with some poetic justice for Dwight's earlier mocking.

jokes.text: Right now? Yes. Here you go. Whoa, no, wait, wait, whoa! Oh, man, I'm sorry. What the [Bleep]? Sorry. Jesus.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to distract Robert from checking his phone
jokes.punchline: Accidentally breaks or damages Robert's phone while trying to delay him
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy mishap but serves the plot well of delaying Robert.

jokes.text: They're not my favorite people in the world. I wouldn't even call them friends. They come over here, eat my pie, dig the crappiest horse grave you've ever seen. God, I'm gonna have to work with them forever, aren't I?
jokes.setup: Dwight reflecting on his coworkers after helping them
jokes.punchline: Resigned realization he's stuck with these incompetent people forever
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Dwight's grudging affection for his coworkers despite insulting them. Good callback to horse grave.

jokes.text: Classic, right? Ooh. I'm all right. You all right? Yep. Skinned knee. Yep.
jokes.setup: Jim and Robert playing squash badly
jokes.punchline: Jim gets injured and tries to play it off casually
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy moment but fits the awkward squash scene well enough.

tmdb_overview: Dwight installs a Doomsday device that will fire all of the employees and effectively close down the branch if they make too many mistakes. Meanwhile, Gabe attempts to court the new warehouse worker, Val.
